title: Chest Pain Emergency
content: Chest pain associated with breathing difficulty may indicate myocardial infarction, pulmonary embolism, or another life-threatening emergency. Immediate medical evaluation is recommended.

title: Fever Assessment
content: Persistent fever lasting more than three days should be evaluated for infection, pneumonia, viral illness, or systemic disease depending on associated symptoms.

title: Headache Evaluation
content: Severe headache lasting more than one week may require neurological evaluation, especially when associated with vomiting, blurred vision, seizures, or weakness.

title: Diabetes Management
content: Patients with diabetes should regularly monitor blood glucose, maintain a healthy diet, exercise regularly, and adhere to prescribed medications.

title: Hypertension
content: Patients with hypertension should monitor blood pressure regularly, reduce dietary sodium, maintain physical activity, and take antihypertensive medications as prescribed.
